summary: Clean audience recording captures an energetic show with a standout China>Rider and lively Sugar Magnolia despite a few uneven moments.
review: The audience‑sourced tape is praised for its clarity and the way it lets listeners hear the crowd, delivering a generally good listening experience. The performance is described as high‑energy and memorable – especially the China Cat Sunflower > I Know You Rider sequence and Bob’s laugh on Sugar Magnolia – though one reviewer found the overall show sloppy and the keyboards phoned‑in.

ai_rating.rationale: Two reviewers rate the show highly (5 stars) while one rates it low (2 stars); recording quality is generally praised but noted as only okay by one reviewer, leading to a weighted average around 4.1.

recording_quality.technical_notes: Clean and crisp sound that lets the crowd be heard; one reviewer calls it "up there with many other great sounding aud's", another says the recording quality is merely okay.

standout_songs: China Cat Sunflower > I Know You Rider
show_quality.setlist_flow: High‑energy performance with strong crowd interaction; a few moments felt sloppy, particularly the keyboard work, but overall the set is remembered as one of the year's top shows.

band_member_comments.Jerry: Forgot the second stanza during China>Rider but otherwise delivered solid guitar work.
band_member_comments.Bob: Laughed onstage during Sugar Magnolia, adding to the fun atmosphere.
band_member_comments.Keys: Peggy O's keyboard sounded phoned‑in and lackluster according to one reviewer.
